article.title: Tensile Strength of Ropes and Friction Hitch Used in Tree Climbing Work

network_boundary.canonical_html_access: A read-only browser renderer returned the complete 493-line article. A shell request received a 403 access-denial body; that denial digest is recorded and was not treated as article content.
network_boundary.official_pdf_asset_access: The official MDPI article PDF asset returned 200 and is the hashed substantive source of record.
network_boundary.doi_resolution: The DOI route returned 302 to the canonical MDPI article URL.

reviewed_regions: article identity, abstract, and copyright statement
complete introduction at purpose and limitation level
all five tables at disposition and essential-number level
all five figures and all other raster occurrences at exclusion level

normalization_and_review_method.pdf_audit_extraction: pypdf 6.14.2 extracted all 12 digital pages with one form-feed separator between pages; used only for completeness, verification, and deterministic audit metrics.
normalization_and_review_method.browser_article_review: The official canonical HTML was reviewed through a read-only browser rendering with 493 addressable lines and crosschecked against the hashed official PDF.
normalization_and_review_method.manual_transformation: Every accepted claim was selected, paraphrased, reorganized, given a section-level source label, and checked for scope. No source extraction, table layout, or figure was emitted as training prose.

rights_review.article_rights_statement: The official article states © 2021 by the authors and distribution under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International.

study_design_audit.factorial_design: two work ropes by two friction hitches
study_design_audit.reported_specimen_structure: three rope pieces and three cords per rope-hitch type, with three traction tests on each described set of sections and cords
study_design_audit.loading_mode: slow pull on semi-static synthetic arborist rope
rope_systems: XTC 16 model: 13 mm in Table 1 and 12.7 mm in later results text, polyamide
measured_outcomes: start and end temperature near the friction hitch

abstract_extrema.rating_status: observed bench extrema only, never a safe load, breaking strength, threshold, or recommendation

factor_effects: hitch type significantly affected load and slip in the reported analysis
rope type significantly affected diameter deformation in the reported analysis
Prusik treatments developed greater grip, higher observed load, and less slip than Valdotain-tresse treatments
XTC 16 showed greater diameter deformation than Axis
results_audit.temperature_status: small start-to-end changes were measured; no significant difference among the four treatments was reported; no dynamic thermal conclusion is permitted
results_audit.purpose_status: preliminary relationship study; authors explicitly did not seek to define the best knot

forbidden_transfers: safe working load or safety factor

component_and_trademark_boundary.retention_reason: These labels are needed to identify the exact experimental factors and are accompanied by a no-endorsement notice.
excluded_classes: third-party literature claims not generated by the experiment

citation_policy.uncited_text_allowed: adaptation notices, conservative transfer limitations, interpretation discipline, and dataset derivation rules

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign the official article HTML identity, official PDF, DOI record, this Markdown, and every derivative to one source-family split before chunking or derivation.
